message:
✨ feat: compliance report generator (PDF/JSON)

Backend:
- pkg/compliance/reports/ — PDF and JSON report generation (zero external deps)
- PDF: cover page, executive summary, per-control findings with evidence
- JSON: kc-compliance-report-v1 schema with full evaluation data
- POST /api/compliance/frameworks/:id/report endpoint
- Content-Disposition headers trigger browser download
- Demo mode generates reports from synthetic evaluation data

Frontend:
- ComplianceReports page with framework/cluster/format pickers
- Uses ui/Select component, useMemo for stable deps
- Dashboard config at /compliance-reports route
- 5 component tests passing

Bug fix:
- Framework GET endpoints (list, detail) now public (no auth required)
- Fixes 'No authentication token available' in demo mode
- POST endpoints (evaluate, report) remain auth-protected

Tests: 6 report package tests + 10 handler tests + 5 frontend tests = 21 total

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+package reports
+
+import (
+	"github.com/kubestellar/console/pkg/compliance/frameworks"
+)
+
+// GenerateDemo produces a report from synthetic evaluation data.
+// Used when no live cluster prober is configured (demo mode).
+func GenerateDemo(fw *frameworks.Framework, cluster, userName string, format ReportFormat) ([]byte, string, error) {
+	result := frameworks.DemoEvaluation(*fw, cluster)
+
+	switch format {
+	case FormatPDF:
+		data, err := GeneratePDF(fw, result, userName)
+		return data, "application/pdf", err
+	default:
+		data, err := GenerateJSON(fw, result, userName)
+		return data, "application/json", err
+	}
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+package reports
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/google/uuid"
+	"github.com/kubestellar/console/pkg/compliance/frameworks"
+)
+
+// GenerateJSON produces a structured JSON compliance report.
+func GenerateJSON(fw *frameworks.Framework, result *frameworks.EvaluationResult, userName string) ([]byte, error) {
+	envelope := ReportEnvelope{
+		SchemaVersion: "kc-compliance-report-v1",
+		ReportID:      uuid.New().String(),
+		GeneratedAt:   time.Now().UTC(),
+		GeneratedBy:   userName,
+		Framework: reportFrameworkSummary{
+			ID:          fw.ID,
+			Name:        fw.Name,
+			Version:     fw.Version,
+			Category:    fw.Category,
+			Description: fw.Description,
+		},
+		Evaluation: result,
+	}
+	return json.MarshalIndent(envelope, "", "  ")
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+// Package reports provides compliance report generation in PDF and JSON formats.
+// It builds on the compliance frameworks evaluation engine to produce downloadable
+// audit-ready reports.
+package reports
+
+import (
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/kubestellar/console/pkg/compliance/frameworks"
+)
+
+// ReportFormat specifies the output format for a compliance report.
+type ReportFormat string
+
+const (
+	FormatPDF  ReportFormat = "pdf"
+	FormatJSON ReportFormat = "json"
+)
+
+// GenerateRequest is the API request body for report generation.
+type GenerateRequest struct {
+	Cluster string       `json:"cluster"`
+	Format  ReportFormat `json:"format"`
+}
+
+// ReportEnvelope wraps an evaluation result with report metadata for JSON export.
+type ReportEnvelope struct {
+	SchemaVersion string                     `json:"schema_version"`
+	ReportID      string                     `json:"report_id"`
+	GeneratedAt   time.Time                  `json:"generated_at"`
+	GeneratedBy   string                     `json:"generated_by"`
+	Framework     reportFrameworkSummary     `json:"framework"`
+	Evaluation    *frameworks.EvaluationResult `json:"evaluation"`
+}
+
+type reportFrameworkSummary struct {
+	ID          string `json:"id"`
+	Name        string `json:"name"`
+	Version     string `json:"version"`
+	Category    string `json:"category"`
+	Description string `json:"description"`
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,282 @@
+package reports
+
+import (
+	"bytes"
+	"fmt"
+	"strings"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/kubestellar/console/pkg/compliance/frameworks"
+)
+
+// pdfDoc is a minimal PDF writer that produces valid PDF 1.4 documents
+// without external dependencies. It builds the cross-reference table
+// and page tree required by the PDF specification.
+type pdfDoc struct {
+	buf     bytes.Buffer
+	offsets []int // byte offset of each object
+}
+
+const (
+	pdfFontSize      = 10
+	pdfTitleFontSize = 18
+	pdfHeaderSize    = 14
+	pdfLineHeight    = 14
+	pdfPageWidth     = 612 // US Letter
+	pdfPageHeight    = 792
+	pdfMargin        = 50
+)
+
+// GeneratePDF produces a PDF compliance report from an evaluation result.
+// It uses a dependency-free PDF writer to avoid external library requirements.
+func GeneratePDF(fw *frameworks.Framework, result *frameworks.EvaluationResult, userName string) ([]byte, error) {
+	doc := &pdfDoc{}
+	lines := buildReportLines(fw, result, userName)
+	pages := paginateLines(lines, pdfPageHeight-2*pdfMargin, pdfLineHeight)
+
+	doc.writeHeader()
+
+	// Object 1: Catalog
+	catalogObj := doc.startObject()
+	doc.buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%d 0 obj\n<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>\nendobj\n", catalogObj))
+
+	// Object 2: Pages (placeholder, updated later)
+	pagesObj := doc.startObject()
+	_ = pagesObj
+
+	// Object 3: Font
+	fontObj := doc.startObject()
+	doc.buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%d 0 obj\n<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>\nendobj\n", fontObj))
+
+	fontBoldObj := doc.startObject()
+	doc.buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%d 0 obj\n<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica-Bold >>\nendobj\n", fontBoldObj))
+
+	// Build page objects
+	pageObjNums := make([]int, 0, len(pages))
+	contentObjNums := make([]int, 0, len(pages))
+
+	for _, page := range pages {
+		// Content stream
+		contentObj := doc.startObject()
+		stream := buildPageStream(page, fontObj, fontBoldObj)
+		doc.buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%d 0 obj\n<< /Length %d >>\nstream\n%s\nendstream\nendobj\n",
+			contentObj, len(stream), stream))
+		contentObjNums = append(contentObjNums, contentObj)
+
+		// Page object
+		pageObj := doc.startObject()
+		doc.buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
+			"%d 0 obj\n<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 %d %d] /Contents %d 0 R /Resources << /Font << /F1 %d 0 R /F2 %d 0 R >> >> >>\nendobj\n",
+			pageObj, pdfPageWidth, pdfPageHeight, contentObj, fontObj, fontBoldObj))
+		pageObjNums = append(pageObjNums, pageObj)
+	}
+
+	// Rewrite pages object
+	kids := make([]string, len(pageObjNums))
+	for i, n := range pageObjNums {
+		kids[i] = fmt.Sprintf("%d 0 R", n)
+	}
+	pagesOff := doc.offsets[1] // object 2 offset
+	_ = pagesOff
+	// We need to rebuild the pages entry
+	doc.rewritePagesObject(pageObjNums)
+
+	return doc.finalize()
+}
+
+type reportLine struct {
+	text   string
+	bold   bool
+	size   float64
+	indent float64
+	spacer float64 // extra space before this line
+}
+
+func buildReportLines(fw *frameworks.Framework, result *frameworks.EvaluationResult, userName string) []reportLine {
+	lines := []reportLine{
+		{text: "COMPLIANCE REPORT", bold: true, size: pdfTitleFontSize, spacer: 10},
+		{text: fw.Name + " " + fw.Version, bold: true, size: pdfHeaderSize, spacer: 8},
+		{spacer: 6},
+		{text: fmt.Sprintf("Cluster: %s", result.ClusterName), size: pdfFontSize},
+		{text: fmt.Sprintf("Generated: %s", time.Now().UTC().Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05 UTC")), size: pdfFontSize},
+		{text: fmt.Sprintf("Generated by: %s", userName), size: pdfFontSize},
+		{spacer: 12},
+		{text: "EXECUTIVE SUMMARY", bold: true, size: pdfHeaderSize, spacer: 4},
+		{spacer: 4},
+		{text: fmt.Sprintf("Overall Score: %d%%", result.Score), bold: true, size: 12},
+		{text: fmt.Sprintf("Total Checks: %d  |  Passed: %d  |  Failed: %d  |  Partial: %d  |  Errors: %d",
+			result.TotalChecks, result.Passed, result.Failed, result.Partial, result.Errors), size: pdfFontSize},
+		{spacer: 12},
+		{text: "DETAILED FINDINGS", bold: true, size: pdfHeaderSize, spacer: 4},
+	}
+
+	for _, ctrl := range result.Controls {
+		lines = append(lines, reportLine{spacer: 8})
+		lines = append(lines, reportLine{
+			text: fmt.Sprintf("[%s] %s — %s (Severity: %s)",
+				strings.ToUpper(string(ctrl.Status)), ctrl.ControlID, ctrl.Title, ctrl.Severity),
+			bold: true, size: pdfFontSize,
+		})
+
+		for _, check := range ctrl.Checks {
+			status := strings.ToUpper(string(check.Status))
+			line := fmt.Sprintf("[%s] %s", status, check.Name)
+			if check.Evidence != "" {
+				line += " — " + check.Evidence
+			}
+			if check.Message != "" {
+				line += " — " + check.Message
+			}
+			lines = append(lines, reportLine{text: line, size: 9, indent: 20})
+		}
+
+		if ctrl.Remediation != "" {
+			lines = append(lines, reportLine{
+				text:   "Remediation: " + ctrl.Remediation,
+				size:   9,
+				indent: 20,
+			})
+		}
+	}
+
+	return lines
+}
+
+func paginateLines(lines []reportLine, usableHeight float64, defaultLineHeight float64) [][]reportLine {
+	var pages [][]reportLine
+	var current []reportLine
+	y := 0.0
+
+	for _, line := range lines {
+		lh := defaultLineHeight
+		if line.size > pdfFontSize {
+			lh = line.size * 1.5
+		}
+		needed := line.spacer + lh
+
+		if y+needed > usableHeight && len(current) > 0 {
+			pages = append(pages, current)
+			current = nil
+			y = 0
+		}
+		current = append(current, line)
+		y += needed
+	}
+	if len(current) > 0 {
+		pages = append(pages, current)
+	}
+	return pages
+}
+
+func buildPageStream(lines []reportLine, fontObj, fontBoldObj int) string {
+	var sb strings.Builder
+	sb.WriteString("BT\n")
+
+	y := float64(pdfPageHeight - pdfMargin)
+
+	for _, line := range lines {
+		y -= line.spacer
+
+		lh := float64(pdfLineHeight)
+		if line.size > pdfFontSize {
+			lh = line.size * 1.5
+		}
+		y -= lh
+
+		if line.text == "" {
+			continue
+		}
+
+		x := float64(pdfMargin) + line.indent
+		font := fontObj
+		if line.bold {
+			font = fontBoldObj
+		}
+		size := line.size
+		if size == 0 {
+			size = pdfFontSize
+		}
+
+		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("/F%d %g Tf\n", font, size))
+		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%g %g Td\n", x, y))
+		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("(%s) Tj\n", escapePDF(line.text)))
+		// Reset position for next absolute placement
+		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%g %g Td\n", -x, -y))
+	}
+
+	sb.WriteString("ET\n")
+	return sb.String()
+}
+
+func escapePDF(s string) string {
+	s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, `\`, `\\`)
+	s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, `(`, `\(`)
+	s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, `)`, `\)`)
+	return s
+}
+
+func (d *pdfDoc) writeHeader() {
+	d.buf.WriteString("%PDF-1.4\n")
+}
+
+func (d *pdfDoc) startObject() int {
+	d.offsets = append(d.offsets, d.buf.Len())
+	return len(d.offsets)
+}
+
+func (d *pdfDoc) rewritePagesObject(pageObjNums []int) {
+	// The pages object is object 2. We stored a placeholder.
+	// In our simple implementation, we write it now.
+	kids := make([]string, len(pageObjNums))
+	for i, n := range pageObjNums {
+		kids[i] = fmt.Sprintf("%d 0 R", n)
+	}
+	pagesContent := fmt.Sprintf("2 0 obj\n<< /Type /Pages /Kids [%s] /Count %d >>\nendobj\n",
+		strings.Join(kids, " "), len(pageObjNums))
+
+	// Replace the placeholder at offsets[1]
+	before := d.buf.Bytes()[:d.offsets[1]]
+	after := d.buf.Bytes()[d.offsets[1]:]
+
+	var newBuf bytes.Buffer
+	newBuf.Write(before)
+	pagesOff := newBuf.Len()
+	newBuf.WriteString(pagesContent)
+
+	// Adjust offsets for objects after the pages object
+	sizeDiff := len(pagesContent) // we inserted, not replaced
+
+	// Find where the next object starts (offset[2] if exists)
+	var restStart int
+	if len(d.offsets) > 2 {
+		restStart = d.offsets[2]
+	} else {
+		restStart = len(d.buf.Bytes())
+	}
+	newBuf.Write(after[restStart-d.offsets[1]:])
+
+	// Update offsets
+	d.offsets[1] = pagesOff
+	for i := 2; i < len(d.offsets); i++ {
+		d.offsets[i] += sizeDiff
+	}
+
+	d.buf = newBuf
+}
+
+func (d *pdfDoc) finalize() ([]byte, error) {
+	// Write xref table
+	xrefOff := d.buf.Len()
+	d.buf.WriteString("xref\n")
+	d.buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("0 %d\n", len(d.offsets)+1))
+	d.buf.WriteString("0000000000 65535 f \n")
+	for _, off := range d.offsets {
+		d.buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%010d 00000 n \n", off))
+	}
+
+	// Trailer
+	d.buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("trailer\n<< /Size %d /Root 1 0 R >>\n", len(d.offsets)+1))
+	d.buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("startxref\n%d\n%%%%EOF\n", xrefOff))
+
+	return d.buf.Bytes(), nil
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,65 @@
+import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest'
+import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react'
+
+// Mock hooks before importing the component
+vi.mock('../../hooks/useComplianceFrameworks', () => ({
+  useComplianceFrameworks: () => ({
+    frameworks: [
+      { id: 'pci-dss-4.0', name: 'PCI-DSS', version: '4.0', description: 'Payment Card Industry', controls: 8, checks: 12 },
+      { id: 'soc2-type2', name: 'SOC 2 Type II', version: '2024', description: 'Service Organization', controls: 4, checks: 8 },
+    ],
+    isLoading: false,
+    error: null as string | null,
+    refetch: vi.fn(),
+  }),
+}))
+
+vi.mock('../../hooks/useMCP', () => ({
+  useClusters: () => ({
+    clusters: [
+      { name: 'prod-cluster' },
+      { name: 'staging-cluster' },
+    ],
+  }),
+}))
+
+vi.mock('../../lib/api', () => ({
+  authFetch: vi.fn(),
+}))
+
+// Import after mocks are set up
+import ComplianceReports from './ComplianceReports'
+
+describe('ComplianceReports', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    vi.clearAllMocks()
+  })
+
+  it('renders header and generator card', () => {
+    render(<ComplianceReports />)
+    expect(screen.getByText('Compliance Reports')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    expect(screen.getByText('Generate Report')).toBeInTheDocument()
+  })
+
+  it('renders framework picker with options', () => {
+    render(<ComplianceReports />)
+    expect(screen.getAllByText(/PCI-DSS/).length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
+  })
+
+  it('renders format buttons', () => {
+    render(<ComplianceReports />)
+    expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /PDF/ })).toBeInTheDocument()
+    expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /JSON/ })).toBeInTheDocument()
+  })
+
+  it('renders generate button', () => {
+    render(<ComplianceReports />)
+    expect(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /Generate & Download Report/ })).toBeInTheDocument()
+  })
+
+  it('renders info section about report types', () => {
+    render(<ComplianceReports />)
+    expect(screen.getByText('PDF Reports')).toBeInTheDocument()
+    expect(screen.getByText('JSON Reports')).toBeInTheDocument()
+  })
+})

@@ -0,0 +1,229 @@ +/** + * ComplianceReports — Generate and download compliance audit reports. + * + * Lets users pick a framework and cluster, choose PDF or JSON format, + * and download a timestamped compliance report. + */ +import { useState, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react' +import { FileText, Download, Shield, Loader2 } from 'lucide-react' +import { useComplianceFrameworks, type Framework } from '../../hooks/useComplianceFrameworks' +import { useClusters } from '../../hooks/useMCP' +import { authFetch } from '../../lib/api' +import { Select } from '../ui/Select' + +type ReportFormat = 'pdf' | 'json' + +export default function ComplianceReports() { + const { frameworks, isLoading: fwLoading } = useComplianceFrameworks() + const { clusters } = useClusters() + const clusterNames = useMemo(() => clusters?.map((c: { name: string }) => c.name) ?? [], [clusters]) + + const [selectedFw, setSelectedFw] = useState('') + const [selectedCluster, setSelectedCluster] = useState('') + const [format, setFormat] = useState('pdf') + const [generating, setGenerating] = useState(false) + const [error, setError] = useState(null) + const [success, setSuccess] = useState(null) + + // Auto-select first framework and cluster when data loads + useEffect(() => { + if (!selectedFw && frameworks.length > 0) { + setSelectedFw(frameworks[0].id) + } + }, [frameworks, selectedFw]) + + useEffect(() => { + if (!selectedCluster && clusterNames.length > 0) { + setSelectedCluster(clusterNames[0]) + } + }, [clusterNames, selectedCluster]) + + const handleGenerate = async () => { + if (!selectedFw || !selectedCluster) return + + setGenerating(true) + setError(null) + setSuccess(null) + + try { + const response = await authFetch(`/api/compliance/frameworks/${selectedFw}/report`, { + method: 'POST', + headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, + body: JSON.stringify({ cluster: selectedCluster, format }), + }) + + if (!response.ok) { + const errBody = await response.json().catch(() => ({ error: 'Download failed' })) + throw new Error(errBody.error || `HTTP ${response.status}`) + } + + // Trigger browser download from response blob + const blob = await response.blob() + const cd = response.headers.get('Content-Disposition') ?? '' + const filenameMatch = cd.match(/filename="?([^"]+)"?/) + const filename = filenameMatch?.[1] ?? `compliance-report.${format}` + + const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob) + const a = document.createElement('a') + a.href = url + a.download = filename + document.body.appendChild(a) + a.click() + URL.revokeObjectURL(url) + document.body.removeChild(a) + + setSuccess(`Report downloaded: ${filename}`) + } catch (err) { + setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Report generation failed') + } finally { + setGenerating(false) + } + } + + const selectedFramework = frameworks.find((f: Framework) => f.id === selectedFw) + + return ( +
+ {/* Header */} +
+

+

{(selectedFramework as Framework & { description?: string }).description || 'Regulatory compliance framework'}

About Compliance Reports
